preview_text:
Colombia enters as the model's favorite with a 49% win probability, substantially ahead of Switzerland's 22%. The gap reflects a meaningful advantage, though the underdog remains far from eliminated at roughly one-in-five odds.

The draw carries notable weight at 30%, indicating the match is expected to be competitive rather than one-sided. Switzerland's path to victory is narrow but plausible; the distribution suggests a match in which all three outcomes retain material probability, with Colombia favored but neither side dominant enough to render alternatives implausible.

The model and market show modest alignment. The sportsbook consensus rates Colombia 6 percentage points lower than the model (43% vs. 49%), while crediting Switzerland 5 points higher (27% vs. 22%). Polymarket prices track closely to the books and similarly favor Colombia by a smaller margin than the model does. The dashboard does not adjudicate which estimate better reflects the true probabilities.
